# CANDIDATE: BEARISH_SWEEP_BLOCKER_NO_QUALITY_LOCK

**Filed:** 2026-05-24  
**Filer:** chef-nemotron (free-tier autonomous R&D)  
**Type:** filter_change  
**Status:** DRAFT (NEEDS-RATIFICATION per Rule 9)

## Hypothesis

Disabling the quality-escalation lock in BEARISH_SWEEP_BLOCKER will isolate the direct P&L impact of sweep-blocked trades, allowing us to measure whether the sweep gate itself is profitable or harmful independent of the cascade effect where blocking a low-quality trade enables a higher-quality entry that later gets blocked by the quality lock.

## Mechanism

The existing BEARISH_SWEEP_BLOCKER filters entries based on sweep conditions (price wicking through a level with high volume) and also includes a quality-escalation lock that prevents same-or-lower quality re-entries after a stop. For this variant, we keep the sweep block active but set `QUALITY_ESCALATION_LOCK_ENABLED = false`. Entry logic: if sweep condition → block; otherwise evaluate quality and enter as per baseline. Exit logic unchanged (TP1 at +30%, runner to breakeven after TP1, etc.). This lets us observe the P&L of trades that would have been blocked by the sweep condition without the subsequent quality-lock cascade.

## OP-20 disclosures

1. **Account-size assumption:** qty=28 requires $25K+; $1K paper ~= 14% headline P&L (per OP-20 disclosure 1).  
2. **Sample bias:** We will test on the full 16-month SPY 5m dataset (Jan 2025–Apr 2026). Selection method: all bars where sweep condition triggers. Overfit risk: disabling one lock may expose other hidden dependencies; we will validate with walk-forward and OOS.  
3. **Out-of-sample:** NEEDS-OOS (will conduct walk-forward held-out window after Stage-1).  
4. **Real-fills:** NEEDS-REAL-FILLS (will re-run top 3 J days through realistic OPRA simulator).  
5. **Failure modes:** Worst day: if sweep block admits many low-quality losses, could increase drawdown; max drawdown scenario: cascade removal may cause consecutive low-quality entries; blow-up scenario: unlocking sweep entries could lead to adverse selection if sweep signals are predominantly noise.  
6. **Concentration:** We will compute top-5 days % of P&L after backtest; unknown until data available.

## Pre-merge gate

<what tests need to pass: gym validators, walk-forward, real-fills>

- Stage-1 gym validators: 67/67 PASS (no regression on entry/exit logic).  
- Walk-forward: OOS/IS Sharpe ratio ≥ 0.5 on 16-month walk-forward (expanding window).  
- Real-fills: P&L diff < ±20% vs BS sim on top 3 J days (4/29, 5/01, 5/04).  
- OP-16 edge_capture ≥ 771 (otherwise REJECT).  

## Confidence

4 / 10 -- We lack empirical data; the hypothesis is plausible given the Dec 10 post-mortem, but the net effect of disabling the quality lock could be positive, negative, or neutral.

## Pre-existing leaderboard impact

This variant does not conflict with any live candidates; it is a diagnostic offshoot of the REJECTED-FINAL BEARISH_SWEEP_BLOCKER. Results may inform whether the sweep gate itself has merit or whether the cascade effect is the sole driver of its rejection. If the sweep block shows positive direct P&L, we may revisit a refined sweep filter that avoids the quality-lock cascade (e.g., time-gated re-entry). If negative, we can deprioritize sweep-based filters altogether. No impact on current leaderboard rankings.
